mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141679k as of May 2015 reflects continued post-crisis employment recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances when PAYEMS month-over-month change is > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no acceleration, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, producing sustained positive equity returns. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when monetary accommodation is confirmed by stable low inflation, creating a dual-condition signal.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 235.982 as of April-May 2015
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.1 million units annualized signal strong residential construction demand, which multiplies through the economy via materials, labor, and consumer wealth effects. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when housing momentum is sustained above trend.
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with rising construction activity
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1100k, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3% or t-test p > 0.15), or correlation between HOUST and subsequent 20d returns is < 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals weakening economic capacity utilization and future earnings headwinds. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, equity investors reprice growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the signal propagates through sentiment and positioning.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with supply-side constraints emerging
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.2% month-over-month, 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > -0.1% or t-test p > 0.20), or the signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show positive momentum simultaneously, the multiplier effect amplifies equity returns through broader economic activity. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both demand channels to be active, producing stronger positive returns than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND HOUST 3-month moving average > 1050k
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444926M and HOUST at 1135k as of May 2015
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than when only one condition is met (interaction coefficient p > 0.10), or the joint signal produces positive returns in < 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected earnings and consumption. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when unemployment crosses above the current level, indicating a regime shift toward slack.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.4pp over 3 months, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0% or t-test p > 0.15), or the signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% annualized rate signals monetary policy tightening pressure, which compresses equity valuations by raising discount rates and reducing real cash flow growth. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansions where inflation becomes the binding constraint on monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.982 as of April 2015 with subdued inflation trajectory
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with inflation acceleration and Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized rate > 2.5%, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or the signal produces negative returns in < 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration below 2% annualized signals weakening aggregate demand and future earnings compression. When quarterly GDP growth slows, equity investors reprice growth expectations downward over the subsequent 60 trading days, producing negative returns as the slowdown propagates through corporate guidance and analyst revisions.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with growth deceleration and recession risk
failure_condition: When GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or the signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
